success_surface: Dynamic multi-column sorting with header-based column detection
effective_behaviors: Dynamic header detection instead of positional indexing
String normalization for robust column name matching
Multi-level sort configuration with explicit priority order
Post-operation verification through shape and content checks
Complete preservation of all columns during transformation
evidence: Column positions detected via iteration over df_original.columns rather than hardcoded indices
Header matching used .strip().lower() to handle whitespace and case variations
sort_values called with list of columns and corresponding ascending flags
Verification read output file back and confirmed shape matches original
All 6 columns preserved in sorted output (no data loss)

memory_items.title: Header-Based Column Identification
memory_items.lesson: Always detect target columns by scanning headers rather than assuming positional indices
memory_items.generalization: When column positions may vary across files, iterate through column names with normalized string matching (strip, case-insensitive) to locate required fields dynamically

memory_items.title: Multi-Level Sort Configuration
memory_items.lesson: For compound sorting, specify all keys as a list with matching order flags
memory_items.generalization: Use list-based sorting parameters where key columns and their sort directions are paired explicitly to maintain priority order and avoid ambiguous sort behavior

memory_items.title: Output Verification Protocol
memory_items.lesson: Always verify transformation results before considering work complete
memory_items.generalization: After data transformations, re-read output files and validate row count, column count, column names, and spot-check sample data to ensure integrity

memory_items.title: Data Integrity Preservation
memory_items.lesson: Ensure all columns remain intact during sorting or filtering operations
memory_items.generalization: Avoid column-selective operations unless explicitly requested; preserve complete row structures when applying row-level transformations like sorting

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Dynamic Header Detection
- **Always scan headers** before accessing columns; never assume positional stability across files.
- **Normalize strings** when matching (strip whitespace, use case-insensitive comparison).
- Iterate through column metadata to find target fields by name pattern.

### Multi-Level Sort Best Practices
- Specify sort keys as an ordered list with corresponding direction flags.
- Verify that primary sort keys are processed before secondary keys.
- Test edge cases: tied values on primary key should resolve correctly on secondary key.

### Output Validation Protocol
- Re-load output files and compare shapes against originals.
- Check column names match expected schema.
- Sample print first/last rows to visually confirm sort order.
- Validate row count remains unchanged after row-only transformations.

### Transformation Safety
- Preserve all columns unless column deletion is explicitly instructed.
- Do not drop index rows or add phantom rows during operations.
- Use `index=False` when exporting to prevent unwanted index columns in Excel output.
